description_long: This is a weathered, industrial control cabinet with a rectangular, upright form and a metallic exterior. The front face features two analog meters, a central rotary dial, and two large switches or circuit breakers, all set into a slightly dented and worn surface. The sides and rear show significant wear, with visible dents, holes, and corrosion, as well as mesh ventilation grilles on the lower sections. The top surface is uneven and damaged, with additional holes and signs of rust, suggesting prolonged outdoor exposure. The color is predominantly a faded, pale greenish-gray, with areas of discoloration and grime. This cabinet is typical of those found in urban infrastructure for electrical or utility control, often seen on sidewalks or in service alleys.
description: A weathered, upright metal control cabinet with analog meters, switches, and mesh ventilation grilles, showing significant wear and corrosion.
description_view_0: Front view with analog meters, rotary dial, and two large switches on a dented metallic surface.
description_view_1: Rear view showing heavy corrosion, holes, and a large mesh ventilation grille at the bottom.
description_view_2: Side view with a plain, dented surface and a single visible hole, minimal features.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with more corrosion, a mesh ventilation grille, and several small protrusions and holes.
